publication.changeSummary: Topic-specific MCQ, fill-gap, and isolated-runner code items awaiting human review before exam use.

items.title: Trace one assignment
items.prompt: Trace these statements in order: balance = 40, spent = 12, balance = balance - spent. Which option describes the stored state immediately after the third statement?

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer is complete, but this AI-assisted draft has not completed independent human review.

hints: Evaluate the expression on the right using the current stored values before replacing the value on the left.

feedback.correct: Correct: the right-hand side reads 40 and 12, computes 28, and assignment replaces balance while spent stays unchanged.
feedback.incorrect: Trace the right-hand side first, then update only the variable named on the left of the assignment.

criteria.description: Identifies the values stored after evaluating and applying the assignment in order.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must confirm that distractors test state replacement, ordered evaluation, and variable independence without ambiguous language.

options.text: balance still stores 40 and spent stores 12 because expressions do not change variables.
Both balance and spent store 28 because they were used in the same expression.
balance stores the unevaluated text balance - spent until it is printed.

answer.explanation: The expression reads the current values 40 and 12, evaluates to 28, and assignment replaces only balance. spent remains 12.

items.title: Complete the state trace
items.prompt: Complete both gaps after tracing balance = 100, balance = balance - 35, refund = 10, and balance = balance + refund.

examEligibility.rationale: The exact accepted values exist, but the item has not completed human review.

hints: Evaluate each right-hand side using values from the immediately preceding state.

feedback.correct: Correct: the purchase leaves 65 and adding the refund produces a final balance of 75.
feedback.incorrect: Rebuild one state-table row per assignment and do not use the final refund before it is assigned.

criteria.description: Calculates the balance immediately after subtracting the purchase amount.
Uses the intermediate balance and refund to calculate the final state.

privateAuthorNotes: Both gaps are required because the item measures ordered state rather than final-output guessing.
items.template: After the purchase, balance is {{after-purchase}}. After assigning refund and adding it, balance is {{final-balance}}.

items.title: Implement two state updates
items.prompt: Complete the Python program so it reads an integer start value, adds 3 to the same named state, doubles that updated value, and prints only the final integer.

examEligibility.rationale: Runner metadata and tests are complete, but formal use remains blocked until independent human approval.

hints: The second update must read the value produced by the first update, not the original input.

feedback.correct: Correct: both assignments update the same state in order before the final value is printed.
feedback.incorrect: Check whether your doubling step reads the value after adding three and whether output contains only the integer.

criteria.description: Applies addition before doubling using the same updated variable state.
Prints exactly the final integer for normal and boundary inputs.

privateAuthorNotes: The negative hidden case detects solutions that concatenate or mishandle the input as text.
items.starterCode:
value = int(input())
# Update value twice, then print it.

answer.referenceSolution:
value = int(input())
value = value + 3
value = value * 2
print(value)
answer.explanation: Assignment first stores start plus three; the next expression reads that updated state, doubles it, and output exposes the final value.
